title: Avere: possession, auxiliary, idioms
grammar_features_introduced: present tense of avere: ho, hai, ha, abbiamo, avete, hanno
the silent h in ho/hai/ha/hanno and its orthographic job (disambiguation from o/ai/a/anno)
possession syntax: subject + avere + direct object
avere as passato-prossimo auxiliary for transitive verbs
no participle agreement with avere (contrast with essere, previewed)
avere + noun idioms: fame, sete, sonno, freddo, caldo, paura, fretta, ragione, torto, bisogno di, voglia di
age expressed with avere, not essere
cognate_patterns_showcased: French avoir + noun ↔ Italian avere + noun idioms
age: j’ai X ans ↔ ho X anni
notes: Fourth A1 grammar lesson, paired with 003-essere-stare. Focuses on avere’s three roles: possession, auxiliary, idiomatic 'be + adjective' collocations. The silent-h section is given its own subsection because it’s a pedagogically load-bearing piece that learners commonly underrate. French speakers get a short 'you already know most of this' paragraph at the end; the main new thing for them is orthographic (writing the silent h).
